The brief facts of the case are that on 24.04.2025 at 06:00 hours
complainant Jingiti Aruna W/o Jangiti Anjaneyulu, Age: 44 years, Caste:
Mudiraj, Occ: Housewife R/o Yellammakunta, Makthal Town, Narayanpet
Ph.9011645665 came to Makthal PS and lodged a Telugu written petition in
which she stated that they have migrated to Bombay for eking livelihood. On
21.04.2025 they came to Makthal for cremation of her husband's father
Jangiti Thayappa. Her husband is the only son to perform final rights to his
father but her sister-in-laws restrained her husband from doing final rights,
--- PAGE BREAK ---
they to subjugate the issue kept silent for that moment. On 23.04.2025 at
:30 hours to 13:00 hours she along with her husband and children went
on the occasion of three days ritual as they were called up on by elders of
their family members, but her sister-in-laws 1.Chityala Sujatha W/o
Ramulu, 2.Latha Koli W/o Amresh and their children 3.Chityala Praveen
S/o Ramulu, 4.Chityala Pramod S/o Ramulu, 5.Prathamesh Koli S/o
Amresh, 6.Vidya Bandari altogether abused them in filthy language and
attacked on them with hands and stones by which they received bleeding
scar injuries, mean while Vidyabandari, Pramod and LathaKoli held her
throat but her brother Neerati Narasimha S/o Venkatramulu intervened and
stopped them, by which they abused him and pushed him aside. They
further threatened them with dire consequences. Hence, requested to take
necessary legal action as per law.
As per the contents of the above complaint, I K Lal Singh, HC 248 of
Makthal PS has registered a Case in Cr.No.100/2025 U/s 126(2), 118(1),
352, 351(2) r/w 3(5) BNS and took up the investigation.
During the course of investigation, I examined and recorded the
statements of LWS-1 & 2 at PS and same incorporated in part-II case diaries
and issued wound certificats, further I was visited the scene of offence,
which is located at house of complainant, Yellammakunta of Makthal town,
there secured the presence of two mediators, in their presence conducted
scene of offence panchanama and drawn rough sketch in crime detail form.
Further I examined and recorded the statements of Lws-3 and 4 in detail in
part-II case diaries then issued to wound certificates to them and referred to
Govt. hospital Makthal.
Further on the same day deputed PC-656 to get the medical
certificates of the victim, as such Dr. Mahesh Raj, CAS, CHC, Makthal
treated them and issued medical certificates in which he opined that the
injuries sustained by victim are "Simple in Nature".
Closed the CD for the day, further progress follows through my next CD.
kroonid Aus
(K Lal Singh, HC-248),
Investigating officer,
MAKTHAL.P.S

Honoured Sir,
This is a case of "Wrongful Restraint, Criminal Intimidation, and Simple Hurt",
which occurred on 23.04.2025 at about 12:30 hours at Yellammakunta, Makthal town. On
the said date and time, the accused persons A1 to A6 wrongfully obstructed and assaulted
the complainant and her family members while they were proceeding to attend the death
ceremony of her uncle. The place of offence is situated at Yellammakunta, Makthal town,
which falls within the jurisdiction of Makthal Police Station and consequently under the
jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court.
The brief facts of the case are that on 24.04.2025 at 06:00 hours complainant Smt.
Jingiti Aruna W/o Jangiti Anjaneyulu, Age: 44 years, Caste: Mudiraj,Occ: Housewife R/o
Yellammakunta, Makthal Town, Narayanpet Ph.9011645665 came to Makthal PS and
lodged a Telugu written petition in which she stated that they have migrated to Bombay
for eking livelihood. On 21.04.2025 they came to Makthal for cremation of her husband's
father JangitiThayappa. Her husband is the only son to perform final rights to his father but
her sister-in-laws restrained her husband from doing final rights, they to subjugate the
issue kept silent for that moment. On 23.04.2025 at 12:30 hours to 13:00 hours she along
with her husband and children went on the occasion of three days ritual as they were
called up on by elders of their family members, but her sister-in-laws 1.Chityala Sujatha
W/o Ramulu, 2.Latha Koli W/o Amresh and their children 3.Chityala Praveen S/o
Ramulu, 4.Chityala Pramod S/o Ramulu, 5.Prathamesh Koli S/o Amresh, 6.Vidya Bandari
altogether abused them in filthy language and attacked on them with hands and stones by
which they received bleeding scar injuries, meanwhile Vidyabandari, Pramod and
LathaKoli held her throat but her brother Neerati Narasimha S/o Venkatramulu
intervened and stopped them, by which they abused him and pushed him aside. They
further threatened them with dire consequences. Hence, requested to take necessary legal
action as per law.
As per the contents of the above complaint, Lw-8 has registered a Case in
Cr.No.100/2025 U/s 126(2), 118(1), 352, 351(2) r/w 3(5) BNS and took up the investigation.
During the course of investigation, LW-8 examined and recorded the statement of
LW-1 to 3 U/s 180(3) of BNSS and incorporated the same in Part-II CD at the Police Station,
--- PAGE BREAK ---
and sent them to Government Hospital, Makthal, for treatment and for issuance of wound
certificate. Subsequently LW-8 visited the scene of offence situated at Yellamma Kunta,
Makthal town which is located towards the North-West direction, at a distance of about 1
kilometer from the Police Station. LW-8 carefully observed the surroundings and secured
two mediators LW-5 and LW-6. In their presence, he conducted the Scene of Offence
Panchanama and prepared a detailed rough sketch of the crime scene in the Crime Detail
Form, depicting all its surroundings accurately. During the panchanama, LW8 searched for
material objects at the scene of the offence, however, no discriminant material relevant to
the crime was found, and therefore, no items were seized at spot. Furthermore, LW-8"
examined and recorded the statement LW-4 U/s 180(3) of BNSS and incorporated the same
in detail in Part-II CDs at the spot.
While efforts were in progress, on 28.4.2025 at 10:00 hours, accused persons A1 to A6
(as mentioned in Column No. 11 of this charge sheet) surrendered before LW-8 at Makthal
PS in connection with this offence. LW-8 served notices U/s 35(3) BNSS to A1 to A6,
informing them of the allegations and directing them to appear for inquiry on or before
29.04.2025 between 10:00 and 18:00 hours.
In compliance, accused A1 to A6 appeared before LW-8 at Makthal PS on 29.04.2025
at 10:00 hours and voluntarily admitted their guilt. LW-8 collected their address proofs,
directed them to appear before the Hon'ble Court as required, and released them, as the
offence is punishable with imprisonment of less than 7 years.
LW-8 received the medical certificate from LW-7/Dr. A. Mahesh Raj, CAS, CHC
Makthal, who treated the injured persons LW-1 to LW-3. In the certificate, he opined that
the injuries sustained by LW-1 to LW-3 are "simple in nature".
Later, LW-9 took over the CD file from LW-8 for further investigation, verified the
investigation already conducted by him, and found it correct and in accordance with
proper legal procedure.
The evidence collected during the investigation, it is well established that LW1
Jangiti Aruna is a resident of Yellammakunta, Makthal, who migrated to Mumbai for
livelihood. The accused persons A1 to A6, hailing from the same village and being
relatives, are involved in the incident. The LWs 2 to 4 are eyewitnesses. The accused A1
and A2 are the own sisters of LW-2. On 21.04.2025, LW-2 along with his family came to
Makthal to attend the death ceremony of his father Chinna Thayappa. At that time, the
accused A1 and A2 obstructed them and told them not to attend the ceremony. Without
quarrelling, they silently returned. On 23.04.2025 at about 12:30 hours, LWs 1 to 4 again
went to attend the three-day ritual program of Chinna Thayappa. At that time, the accused
A1 to A6 altogether obstructed them, abused them in filthy language, and assaulted them
with hands and stones. As a result, they sustained bleeding injuries and scar injuries.
Further, the accused A3 to A6 caught hold of LW-1's throat and threatened her with dire
consequence. In the meantime, LW-4 intervened and tried to rescue them, but the accused
also beat him with hands. Thus, the accused A1 to A6 committed offences punishable
under Sections 126(2), 118(1), 352, 351(2) r/w 3(5) BNSS.
--- PAGE BREAK ---
Therefore, the Hon'ble court is prayed that the accused persons mentioned in
column No. 11 of this charge sheet may be tried and dealt suitably as per law.
Hence the charge sheet.
